InformedConsent:
Welcome to the research study!  

 

We are interested in better understanding the nature of dreams.  You will be presented with information relevant to the self-reporting of dreams and some mental health measures. Please be assured that your responses will be kept completely confidential. This survey is one of a series of five. If you are reading this, you completed our initial survey and first two follow ups, and this is regarding the third follow-up study 1 month later. Following participation in this survey, you will be invited to the last follow-up survey in 1 month's time – all your responses will be linked so that trends can be tracked over time. You will be paid for each follow-up survey. Following the completion of all surveys, there will be a $1.00 bonus. 

The survey should take 10 minutes to complete. After completing the survey, you will be paid $2.00 for your participation. The final survey with the bonus ($1.00 + $2.00) will pay you $3.00 for a total of $12.00. We do not ask for your name or any other information that might identify you. Although collected data may be made public or used for future research purposes, your identity will always remain confidential.


The research will not benefit you personally. We know of no risks resulting from participating in the study. Your participation is voluntary. You may withdraw at any time, and you may choose not to answer any question, but you must proceed to the final screen of the study in order to receive your completion code which you must submit in order to be paid. In accordance with Prolific policies, we may reject your work if the HIT was not completed correctly or the instructions were not followed. Your participation in this research is voluntary. You have the right to withdraw at any point during the study, for any reason, and without any prejudice. 

If you have any questions you can contact me at pcm38@duke.edu, or my advisor at ws66@duke.edu. If you have questions about your rights as a research subject, contact Duke’s Campus Institutional Review Board at campusirb@duke.edu or at 919-684-3030, please refer to protocol #2024-0062. By clicking the button below, you acknowledge that your participation in the study is voluntary, you are 18 years of age, and that you are aware that you may choose to terminate your participation in the study at any time and for any reason.

DreamMemory: II’ve had a bunch of pretty vivid dreams in the past month but Ive noticed that almost all of them I seem to forget right after waking up.  One dream last weekend though  I was able to remember and I actually wrote some notes to help me recall.  I was at “home” in the dream but it wasn’t my home.  I was living there obviously in the dream but it wasn’t my current house or any place that I had ever stayed.  It was a large house, 2 stories but with almost no furniture.  The really weird and memorable part was that while I was in this house, there were groups of people that I didn’t know walking through the house, almost like they were visiting an open house with a realtor.  These people didn’t acknowledge me and I didn’t acknowledge them either.  I remember feeling a bit uneasy during the dream but it wasn’t a feeling of outright fear or terror.

So I decided to go to some of the groups on earth to think through the decision. The first group was very religious (I’m an atheist) so I didn’t find it helpful. Then I saw one that was described as an athletic group. I’m very active so I thought that would be a good fit. DreamMemory:
So, I was in a large building, kind of like a mall. I was running with my cane (disabled in real life too) away from this thing that looks like my little sister but is not my little sister. This look-alike is a recurring theme in some of my past dreams over the years. Anyway, she was chasing after me with a spider that looked like a recluse spider. I was going from room to room, hallway to hallway, running for my life. When I finally thought I lost her, she came in from the ceiling with that spider in my face trying to get me to hold it. I hit her with my cane and bolted down another endless stream of hallways until I finally got to a set of very steep stairs that I struggled to climb up. When that look-alike got to the bottom of the stairs, I yelled, "If you come any closer, I will whack the shit out of you!" 
Then she stopped trying to go up the stairs and said, "Fine, but there's always a next time." which she wasn't wrong because this isn't the first time I've seen her. 

After that, I decided to continue to walk up the steep set of stairs and found a door that led to a waiting room. There were a lot of people waiting there with a lot of stuff on the ground. Books, bookbags, water bottles, and things like that were scattered around. I saw some familiar faces like people I met in middle school or high school but there were a lot of faceless people I couldn't recognize. 

Since everyone was waiting on these plastic chairs that were connected together, I thought I should do the same but everyone started to get up and go toward the back where there was a set of doors that led into a movie theater. I decided to follow the crowd to not look out of place and I found myself a seat in the middle of the movie theater but closer to the edge so it's easier for me to leave. 

The movie that was playing was an animation but it was a horror mystery that was made in very bright and vibrant colors. As I was watching, someone walked by with drinks and asked me to pick one. I picked one that looked like coke but the can was similar to an energy drink can (skinny). Then the person was like "Put out your hand." and I was like "Why?" but I did anyway. And they poured Sprite into the palm of my hand and stared at it intently for several seconds before finally saying, "Okay, you are good to go." 
I was like, "What do I do with this?" Then they were like, "Figure it out." Luckily, someone gave me a napkin to clean up my hand and they gave me the opened can of Sprite. For some reason, I didn't want to drink it even though I know I am notorious for eating and drinking in my dreams. 

After that, I continued to watch the movie with the drinks in my hand and I heard someone or something say, "And where are we? What is all of this?" 

I then said, "This is an illusion, well, more like a dream." with no hesitation. 

After that. everything around me started to crumble and I was left in the ruins of this movie theater that looked like it had been abandoned for decades. 

Almost everyone around me disappeared except for a figure sitting where the large screen should have been. It looked like he was sitting on a throne. He sat up, looked at me but with a frown, and said, "I didn't think you would notice. You would have been trapped in this 'illusion' forever with no way to return..." 

I quickly woke up, gasping for air like I was holding my breath the whole time.
More_DreamList: Flying,Falling,Death-murder,Natural disaster (earthquake, war, floods, etc.),Eating delicious foods,Snakes or insects,Being chased,Dreams of intense colors,Strong negative emotions

DreamMemory:
It started in the finished basement TV room of my childhood home. I was behind the bar, carefully wiring up a makeshift television transmitter to my computer to allow my cousin and I to watch something on the TV because nothing good happened to be on cable. I forget exactly what we were watching, but we made note that the analog reception was actually rather poor depending upon where I stood. I had the idea to take the antenna that I normally use for radio reception and hook it up and that seemed to improve things substantially. We decided to fall asleep on the futon instead, at least until more people arrived because it turned out that we were celebrating Easter. We were having dinner and it was notable primarily because we were having store-bought fried chicken and copious quantities of cheap alcohol -- totally out of character for these sorts of things -- while tending to an outdoor fire pit that was placed on the counter next to the stove for some reason. A fire also roared in the fireplace in the living room just around the corner. Before we left to engage in other activities I remember vividly shoving handfuls of chicken legs into my pockets from a bucket, and throwing another piece onto the fire. We then cut to us exchanging information about how to use Mastodon, and posting ridiculous pictures. We are sure to remain in contact we say, as we head off to university and then cut once more. 

Now I'm playing the sims on a Macbook, using some sort of bizarre mod to alter the characters' motivations in extremely vivid detail. It was an abusive, dysfunctional family marred by alcoholism. I was trying to get the husband to start drinking again after a period of sobriety that had led his battered wife to return. I succeeded. They were drinking straight from the bottle before I knew it. Then the game froze. It wasn't a legal copy, and it was specifically the Sims 3; I know this because the file had trouble halfway through and I realized that my NAS was shut down and I ran to the closet to restart it. The game was salvaged, but only partly. The floor fell out from the characters and they descended into a literal hell. Fighting to escape demons through a corn maze on one level before falling into lava below. Only to repeat the process. It was rather disturbing, so I closed the game and explored the computer a bit more. It was outdated for this model of macbook -- something from the OS X days -- and it was bizarre because now I sat among my own family and no matter what I did pornographic videos kept starting up of men in various states of undress. I close the computer and start running out the door. I'm being chased as I do so. I enter a computer lab and start typing -- I realize I'm some sort of computer hacker -- as the police show up and open fire. 

But I'm not dead. Now we cut to a more rural but still nominally-suburban scene. I'm running about with people I'm not sure I can trust. We enter an abandoned house through the garage, flipping circuit breakers until a long-haired man walks out the door and directs us toward a hill. One of the people I'm with trips, a packet of cigarettes falls out, and I go to grab one and smoke it. The long-haired man warns us not to smoke. I ignore it. At this point a group chases me down and takes me to the top of the hill. There is a large wooden structure, with a rope hanging from the top. It is announced that I am to suffer "the punishment". It is not a mere hanging, though. It's proper torture. I'm lifted up to the top after being completely bound totally immobile and more ropes appear, swinging objects towards me. Searing pain erupts; the last I remember is a red-hot spiky wrecking ball thing and in my mind I'm wondering how to block it out until I finally die. And then I wake up.
DreamQ_Frequency: Three or more times a week
